title: May a woman uncover her hair for a man who is proposing to her before the nikah?
tags: can man see woman hair before nikah
stanceSummary: Hanafi, Maliki, and Shafi'i generally require full hijab before nikah even in supervised meetings. The Hanbali school (with evidence such as the hadith encouraging looking at a prospective spouse) allows her to appear before the suitor as she would before her mahrams (e.g., hair uncovered in modest clothing) if she chooses—not forced. Meetings should always be with mahrams present.
alternateQuestions: Can fiancé see hair before marriage?
Is it halal to show hair to potential husband?
transcript:
## Summary of Yasir Qadhi's Position

In a [2020 video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pGwTrQ8U7Bs) (starting at [0:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pGwTrQ8U7Bs&t=0)), Yasir Qadhi addresses this question.

- **Three madhabs**: hijab stays as with any non-mahram until nikah.
- **Hanbali view**: may uncover hair in front of the suitor **in mahrams’ presence**, analogous to dress before father/brother—not obligatory.
- Seeing a spouse **before marriage** is encouraged; **khilwa** (privacy) remains forbidden.

---
